rationale: The US-Iran ceasefire announcement and falling oil prices are the main story for Romania, offering potential energy cost relief, but the deal remains unconfirmed/unsigned — continuation of last cycle's baseline with no new qualitative change.

top_events.title: US-Iran announce ceasefire/peace deal; oil prices fall
top_events.summary: Falling oil prices from the US-Iran deal could ease Romania's energy import costs, but the deal is not yet signed — continuation of last cycle's development with no new qualitative leap.

top_events.title: Stock markets soar on US-Iran deal confirmation
top_events.summary: Global market gains offer mild indirect benefit to Romanian financial markets and investment climate, but this is a continuation with no new qualitative change.

top_events.title: Lebanese remain sceptical despite US-Iran ceasefire announcement
top_events.summary: Regional scepticism about the deal's durability is relevant to Black Sea security context but has no direct impact on Romanian daily life.
